source.title: American Big English 2 Student's Book
source.purpose: Private curriculum extraction for an original, unofficial interactive practice website.
source.copyright_boundary: This package records syllabus facts, short target language patterns, vocabulary lists, and pedagogical structure. It intentionally excludes scanned pages, publisher images, audio, songs, long stories, and substantial verbatim exercises.

course.description: An original interactive practice companion aligned to the Big English 2 learning progression.
course.target_level: Primary beginner ESL; approximately pre-A1 to early A1
course.delivery: Static browser application deployed through GitHub Pages

units.title: Welcome to Class!
units.theme: Classroom objects, family members, everyday actions, and review language.

speaking: ask and answer about current actions
writing: complete short location and action sentences

outcomes: Name common classroom objects and close family members.
Use on and under to describe location.
Ask and answer about actions happening now.
Use does ... have? with short answers.
Distinguish singular and plural location questions.

units.title: In My Classroom
units.theme: Classroom activities and describing what people are doing.
units.story_theme: Classmates identify different children who share the same first name by observing their classroom activities.

content_connection.subject: Math
content_connection.title: Arithmetic
concepts: read and solve simple addition and subtraction statements
use English number words up to 100

culture_connection.title: Classes outside the classroom
concepts: school activities can happen in forests, gardens, mountains, and boats

values.title: Take turns
values.project: Create a simple puppet and practise a turn-taking role-play.

outcomes: Use words for classroom activities.
Use there is and there are to describe a classroom.
Ask and answer about what people are doing in the classroom.

units.title: My Games
units.theme: Outdoor activities, preferences, positions, and safe play.

patterns: What does she like to do?
What do they like to do?

units.story_theme: Children discover how different sports and skills can be combined when they play as a team.

content_connection.subject: Science
content_connection.title: Bones and muscles
concepts: bones and muscles help the body move
different actions use hands, feet, and many muscles

values.title: Play safely
concepts: wear a helmet and pads
sit on swings
keep feet in front on a slide

outcomes: Use words for outdoor activities.
Say what I like to do.
Say what other people like to do.

speaking: ask what people like to do

units.title: In My House
units.theme: Rooms, furniture, family relationships, and object location.

patterns: My mother's phone is on the dresser.
Ben's keys are on the table.

units.story_theme: Visiting cousins move through different rooms, prompting questions about people and objects in the house.

content_connection.subject: History
content_connection.title: Old and new things at a museum
concepts: compare older and modern household or technology objects

culture_connection.title: Household objects around the world
concepts: clay cooling pots
solar ovens
low chairs
hammocks

values.title: Be neat
values.project: Make a toy box and practise sorting household items.

outcomes: Use words for rooms, furniture, and objects in the house.
Ask and answer about where things are.

units.title: In My Town
units.theme: Places in town, purposes, maps, transportation, and street safety.

patterns: I want to mail a letter.
She wants to go to the park.
Is there a post office near here?
Is there a bank on Elm Street?

units.story_theme: A child and parent use a map to locate a bookstore and other town places.

content_connection.subject: Geography
content_connection.title: Transportation around the world
concepts: travel to school by boat, bus, subway, or bike
compare fast, slow, safe, and active transport

values.title: Cross the street safely
sequence: look left, right, and left again

outcomes: Use words for places in town.
Use want/wants to with action words.
Say what I want to do.
Ask and answer about places in town.

speaking: state a purpose using want to

units.title: My Dream Job
units.theme: Jobs, ambitions, interests, goods, services, and setting goals.

patterns: What do you want to be?
I want to be an actor.
What does she want to be?
She wants to be a doctor.
I want to be a vet because I like animals.

units.story_theme: Children discuss dream jobs and connect each job with an activity they enjoy.

content_connection.subject: Social Science
content_connection.title: Goods and services
concepts: some jobs produce goods
some jobs provide services
some businesses do both

culture_connection.title: Unusual jobs around the world
concepts: jobs can differ because of local geography, transport, and customer needs

values.title: Study hard and set goals
values.project: Make a flip book about a future job, interests, and goals.

outcomes: Use job names.
Say what I want to be and why.
Ask and answer about what people want to be.

speaking: say a dream job and why

units.title: My Day
units.theme: Clock time, daily routines, work schedules, and punctuality.

patterns: He gets up at six o'clock.
When do you go to bed?
I go to bed at eight o'clock.

units.story_theme: A child compares their daily schedule with a pet's unusual sleep and activity pattern.

content_connection.subject: History
content_connection.title: Telling the time
concepts: people used shadows, water, sand, and candles to measure time

culture_connection.title: Different working hours
concepts: different jobs begin and finish at different times

values.title: Be on time
language: I get my backpack ready the night before school.
I get up early on school days.
I get dressed quickly and eat breakfast.
I always get to school on time.
values.project: Make a My Day book with clocks and routine pictures.

outcomes: Use o'clock to tell the time.
Use the present simple to describe activities.
Talk about a daily routine with time expressions.
Ask and answer about daily routine activities.

units.title: My Favorite Food
units.theme: Food preferences, healthy choices, snacks, and fruit around the world.
units.story_theme: Family members choose an afternoon snack and discuss which fruits they like.

content_connection.subject: Science
content_connection.title: Healthy and unhealthy snacks
concepts: read simple food-label information
recognize too much fat, salt, or sugar

culture_connection.title: Where fruit comes from
concepts: fruits grow in different climates and regions

outcomes: Use words for types of food.
Use like/likes and don't/doesn't like with food words.
Say what food I like and don't like.
Ask and answer about food preferences.

speaking: ask and answer about favorite foods
writing: write a short healthy snack plan

units.title: Wild Animals
units.theme: Wild animals, abilities, habitats, helping animals, and appreciation.
units.editorial_note: The contents table lists 'parrot', while the Unit 8 vocabulary page labels 'peacock'. Use peacock as the primary taught word and retain parrot as an extended/checkpoint word.
units.story_theme: Children explain why they like particular animals and what those animals can do.

content_connection.subject: Science
content_connection.title: Animal habitats
concepts: animals live in habitats that provide food, water, and cover

culture_connection.title: Helping animals
concepts: people care for injured, rescued, or working animals in different places

values.title: Appreciate animals
values.project: Make an animal-and-habitat map for the learner's country.

outcomes: Use words for wild animals.
Use can/can't with action words.
Ask and answer questions about what animals can do.

speaking: give a reason for liking an animal
writing: write a short animal fact card

units.title: Fun All Year
units.theme: Months, seasons, annual routines, celebrations, and active lifestyles.

patterns: He always has a New Year's party in January.
We never go on vacation in the winter.
What does he do in January?
Do you go on vacation in the winter?

units.story_theme: Children compare favorite months, vacations, birthdays, and activities they always or never do.

culture_connection.title: New Year's Eve
concepts: foods, songs, visits, and sounds are used in different New Year traditions

outcomes: Use words for months, seasons, and celebrations.
Use always or never to describe regular activities.
Talk about important activities and events during a year.
Ask and answer what people do at different times of year.

starters_practice.title: Original Starters-style Practice
starters_practice.rule: Create new questions and new illustrations. Do not reproduce official or book practice items.

task_families.type: write a name or number from a scene
unscramble letters to write a pictured word
choose words from a box to complete a short text
large scene description and examiner-style prompts
